method: AST pass over all 127 modules of src/fanops (scratchpad/c3/census.py), classifying every ExceptHandler by what its BODY does — re-raise / raise-new / log / return-None / return-[] / return-{} / return-False / return-a-value / pass / continue / state-change. Plus targeted manual tracing of every handler on the publish, reconcile, gate and recovery paths.

census.note_on_the_80: ~60 of these are `return ActionResult(ok=False, ...)` — EXPLICIT failures surfaced to the operator, NOT silent swallows. Counting them as defects would be dishonest.

house_norm: fail-open with a LOGGED breadcrumb. Enforced in CI by tests/test_swallow_ratchet.py, which fails a NEW silent broad `except`. The 49 silent handlers are the grandfathered baseline plus contract-correct exceptions. I did NOT classify all 331 broad catches as defects; each was judged by its CONTRACT and its DOWNSTREAM INTERPRETATION.

findings.title: A post with a REAL submission_id has NO terminal path in reconcile
findings.reachability: reachable on BOTH backends, no flag required
findings.what_failed: Nothing failed — the post is simply never resolved. Both the XC-1 escalation (reconcile.py:746) and the XC-2 give-up (reconcile.py:757) are gated on `_is_fake_token(post)`. A post carrying a REAL backend submission id is structurally excluded from BOTH.
findings.preconditions: a post reaches submitting/needs_reconcile carrying a real submission_id AND the backend never reports published/failed for it (platform deleted the post, integration removed, or Postiz reports its own QUEUE state forever -> _POSTIZ_STATE_MAP maps unknowns to 'scheduled')
findings.caller_can_distinguish: NO — _publish_one returns the string 'submitting', a success-shaped value, after doing nothing (the skip-resubmit path, run.py:287). publish_due counts it as neither published nor failed; it vanishes from the tally.
findings.retry_repeats_side_effect: no — the POST is skipped entirely
findings.recovery: NONE, autonomous or manual. The Studio requeue family accepts ONLY failed/error, so a `submitting` post is not offered. The only escape is `fanops resolve <id> failed` then Studio recover — a two-step CLI break-glass requiring the operator to ALREADY KNOW the post is stranded, and nothing tells them.
findings.operator_signal: one `stuck ...` breadcrumb stamped ONCE at 6h, then NEVER updated. A post stuck 3 days and one stuck 3 years look IDENTICAL.
findings.worst_credible_consequence: A post the operator believes is publishing sits in `submitting` indefinitely. It is never published, never failed, never surfaced as terminal, and never counted in the `gave_up` digest bucket. Silent non-delivery.
findings.mitigation_is: accidental — the breadcrumb is a log line, not a state, and reconcile.py:767 uses its OWN presence to suppress all further attention
findings.evidence: EXP-4/H6 (executed): a real-token post on the WORKING Postiz client shape held `submitting` at +6h, +25h, +73h and +100000h. No escalation, no give-up.
findings.code_justification_is_an_assumption: reconcile.py:76 — 'A post carrying a real id is left to its normal poll (its status WILL resolve), never escalated.' That is an ASSUMPTION, not a guarantee, and it is the load-bearing one.

findings.title: The XC-1 escalation and XC-2 give-up are DEAD CODE on the Zernio backend
findings.reachability: every TikTok/Zernio post; no flag required
findings.what_failed: The escalation and give-up live in the `else:` branch that runs ONLY after a SUCCESSFUL poll (reconcile.py:739). A poll that RAISES is caught at reconcile.py:627 and `continue`s at :635 — it never reaches them. ZernioStatusClient.get_status RAISES RuntimeError on any non-2xx INCLUDING a 404. PostizStatusClient.get_status instead RETURNS {'status':'unknown'} — which is the ONLY reason the machinery works at all.
findings.root_cause: A SIBLING-PARITY DIVERGENCE between the two status clients, exactly the class src/fanops/CLAUDE.md warns about. The code's own comment at reconcile.py:617-618 says 'Polling it 404s -> the status client's get_status raises RuntimeError' — the author KNEW it raises, and still placed the escalation where a raise cannot reach.
findings.operator_signal: a `reconcile poll error: ...` line every pass, forever. The `gave_up` digest bucket — the system's best failure surface — NEVER FIRES ON ZERNIO.
findings.worst_credible_consequence: Every crash-stranded or malformed-provider TikTok post is permanently unlabeled. This also RETRO-CORRECTS Cycle-2's F-A blast radius, which asserted such a post is 'eventually labeled' — true on Postiz, FALSE on Zernio.
findings.evidence: EXP-4/H5 (executed): the SAME fanops_-token post, driven with the Zernio client's raising shape, held `submitting` at +6h, +25h, +73h, +1000h and +100000h.

findings.title: Unbounded temp-dir leak — fanops-shrink-* is never cleaned
findings.what_failed: compress.maybe_shrink_for_cap does tempfile.mkdtemp(prefix='fanops-shrink-', dir=cfg.base/'04_agent_io') and NOTHING in the tree ever removes it. It is the ONLY mkdtemp in src/fanops; a repo-wide grep for rmtree finds only transcribe.py's demucs/keyframe cleanup.
findings.recovery: operator, by hand — and there is no signal telling them to
findings.operator_signal: NONE. No log, no metric, no doctor check, no wipe path.
findings.worst_credible_consequence: unbounded disk growth under 04_agent_io/, one dir + one re-encoded mp4 per oversize upload, forever
findings.evidence: EXP-10 (executed): a 5 MiB fixture through apply_shrink_to_post(backend='zernio') left 04_agent_io/fanops-shrink-c9zxj7_0/clip_1.crf28.mp4 behind.

findings.title: Render.path is durably rewritten INTO a mkdtemp directory
findings.what_failed: apply_shrink_to_post sets led.renders[rid] = r.model_copy(update={'path': str(shrunk)}) where `shrunk` lives in the mkdtemp dir, and _publish_one FINALIZE PERSISTS it. The ledger's canonical durable media pointer for a shrunk render is a TEMP-DIR PATH.
findings.recovery: paths_rebase (paths_rebase.py:75) can rewrite Render.path, but only for a root move — it does not know about temp dirs
findings.worst_credible_consequence: any cleanup of 04_agent_io (a tmpwatch, an operator freeing disk, a future GC) dangles the render pointer with NO invalidation rule. Compounds C3-F4: the fix for the leak would BREAK the pointer.
findings.existing_mitigation: Config.render_path is documented as 'ALWAYS under self.base so the Studio _bounded serve check passes' — 04_agent_io IS under base, so the media route still serves. Accidental.
findings.supersedes: MUTATION_MATRIX.md A.3, which described the write as 'a post-compression path rewrite' without naming what it is rewritten TO

findings.title: A torn attempts.json silently resets the gate-retry ceiling
findings.what_failed: bump_attempts uses a bare p.write_text(...) — NOT the tmp + os.replace that its two siblings IN THE SAME FILE (write_request :63-66, write_response :82-87) use. A torn attempts.json hits `except Exception: n = 0` and the counter restarts.
findings.caller_can_distinguish: NO — a reset counter is indistinguishable from a fresh gate
findings.poison_pill: yes, weakly — the bounded 3-attempt escalation (_GATE_DETERMINISTIC_MAX, responder.py:53) becomes UNBOUNDED, so a permanently-failing `moments` gate never promotes its source to SourceState.error
findings.worst_credible_consequence: a gate retries the LLM forever instead of quarantining its source
findings.evidence: EXP-7 (executed): bump_attempts reached n=3 (the ceiling), a torn write was simulated, the next call returned n=1.
findings.falsifies: brief section 9 claim #8 ('every control-file write is crash-safe')

findings.title: note_stage is a torn-write window on .run.lock
findings.what_failed: os.ftruncate(fd, 0) then os.write(fd, ...) — no os.replace. A crash in the window empties the lock body.
findings.caller_can_distinguish: NO — _read_body's broad except returns {}, which is identical to 'no stage recorded yet'
findings.recovery: the FLOCK (the authority) is kernel-released on process death, so the LEASE self-heals. Only the advisory body is lost.
findings.operator_signal: run_stage_snapshot -> None; daemon.status loses its stage-stuck detection input
findings.worst_credible_consequence: degraded mid-pass observability; NOT a correctness break
findings.existing_mitigation: the flock/body split is deliberate and documented (pipeline_run.py:5-6)

findings.title: _requeue_transient_failed_for_daemon: the only log-free swallow in post/run.py
findings.reachability: a LockBusyError or commit failure during the requeue txn
findings.what_failed: `except Exception: return requeued`. `requeued` is incremented INSIDE the transaction, so a commit failure returns a NON-ZERO count while NOTHING persisted.
findings.caller_can_distinguish: NO — but publish_due (run.py:440) DISCARDS the return value, so no caller is currently lied to
findings.recovery: the next tick retries the requeue
findings.worst_credible_consequence: nil today; a latent trap for any future caller that trusts the count

findings.title: The daemon re-drives an unrecoverable AuthError every tick, forever, with no heartbeat
findings.reachability: a rotated / revoked Postiz or Zernio key
findings.what_failed: AuthError correctly halts the PASS (INV-11 holds — the queue is never burned). But _cmd_run_pass catches it, prints to stderr, and returns None; the daemon's own `except Exception` then swallows and sleeps. The heartbeat is emitted ONLY when s is not None, so NO heartbeat is written. Nothing changes in the ledger.
findings.caller_can_distinguish: the stderr line goes to the plist's StandardErrorPath
findings.operator_signal: stderr only — AND daemon.status still reports `alive` (see C3-F9)
findings.worst_credible_consequence: publishing stops entirely and the system's own liveness command says everything is fine
findings.existing_mitigation: the Studio Home banner does an INDEPENDENT live GET and DOES surface the 401
findings.mitigation_is: structural, but on a DIFFERENT surface than the one the operator would check

findings.title: daemon.status reports `alive` while every pass halts
findings.what_failed: status deliberately overrides a stale heartbeat when daemon_progress reports alive_mid — 'the newest run.log line of ANY kind is younger than the ceiling'. A failing pass STILL writes run.log lines (every stage logs before the halt), so alive_mid is True and the verdict is `alive` — while no heartbeat has been written in hours. heartbeat_age_s IS in the returned dict, but 'no longer governs the verdict on its own'.
findings.caller_can_distinguish: the data is present; the VERDICT discards it
findings.worst_credible_consequence: the operator asks the system's own liveness question and is told `alive` while nothing publishes
findings.existing_mitigation: the intent is correct and documented ('that heartbeat only lands after a whole pass finishes, so it must NEVER flip a fast-logging pass to stale'). The CONSEQUENCE was never recorded.
findings.mitigation_is: structural for its stated purpose; the gap is that 'alive' conflates 'the process is running' with 'the work is succeeding'
findings.does_not_contradict: project memory `liveness-verdict-single-owner` — this QUALIFIES what the single owner can and cannot tell you

findings.title: adjust.retire — the only IRREVERSIBLE learning actuator — has the WEAKEST gate
findings.reachability: any live backend with >=3 analyzed posts
findings.what_failed: _learn_pass calls retire(led, r['losers']) directly, gated ONLY on cfg.is_live_backend. adjust.retire writes MomentState.retired (adjust.py:95), and reconcile_moments REFUSES to un-retire (ledger.py:636-642) — it is IRREVERSIBLE. It is NOT behind validation_gate.learning_validated, and it fires as soon as round(n*0.2) >= 1, i.e. n=3 analyzed posts. Meanwhile the REVERSIBLE amplify-side bias actuators wait for learning_validated AND p4_unlocked (>=8 attributed posts x >=2 values, validation_gate.py:52).
findings.recovery: NONE — the retirement is structurally irreversible
findings.operator_signal: per-item only; the moment disappears from the render pool
findings.worst_credible_consequence: a moment lineage is permanently suppressed on 3 data points
findings.existing_mitigation: the guards ARE real: a loser must be in the bottom 20% AND below lift_floor=20.0 AND not a winner AND NOT lift_degraded (adjust.py:50-52). This is why it is a hazard, not a reachable defect.
findings.does_not_retract: INV-14, which is TRUE as scoped (p4_dim_bias / variant_amplify / timing_bias). The scope simply hides the destructive path.

findings.title: The F44 upload cache has no invalidation rule
findings.reachability: any hosted media URL that expires
findings.what_failed: Clip.media_url / Render.media_url are written first-writer-wins and NOTHING ever invalidates them. If the hosted URL expires, the dead URL is reused forever.
findings.recovery: operator must clear the field by hand
findings.operator_signal: the publish would fail at the backend with an unfetchable media URL
findings.confidence: HIGH (static; not executed against an expiring CDN)

findings.title: framing._detect_faces returns PARTIAL results on an exception
findings.what_failed: `except Exception: return out` returns the partially-accumulated face list. A crash after 3 of 5 faces is indistinguishable from 'there were 3 faces' — which can classify a real 2-shot as a 1-shot and pick the wrong crop.
findings.existing_mitigation: deliberate ('a single bad frame never sinks the window'); the whole framing path is fail-open to a centre-crop

findings.title: A lost audit line is silent and unrecoverable
findings.what_failed: `except Exception: pass` — and write_audit is called AFTER the ledger transaction commits (e.g. actions.py:1008/1040/1070), so a crash between commit and audit loses the audit line while KEEPING the state change. Audit is a RECORD, not a JOURNAL.
findings.worst_credible_consequence: the record of a state change vanishes
findings.existing_mitigation: CONTRACT-CORRECT and explicitly documented: 'NEVER raises: the caller's action MUST complete even if the audit write fails ... The log is a tail-and-grep surface, not load-bearing for correctness.' Losing an audit line is strictly better than failing an approve.
findings.note: Filed as a GAP, not a defect. The design decision is right.

findings.title: NON-DEFECT — the publish retry loop cannot double-POST
findings.hypothesis_tested: _publish_one's outer `for attempt in range(3)` loop re-calls poster.publish on a transient error, so a ReadTimeout AFTER the body was sent would re-POST and double-publish.
findings.verdict: DISCONFIRMED. poster.publish NEVER lets a transient escape: it retries ONLY ConnectTimeout (connection never established => body never sent) and 429 (body rejected), and converts EVERY other network exception and EVERY 5xx into a RETURNED ledger with state=needs_reconcile. The only exception that escapes is AuthError, which _is_fatal_auth_error re-raises FIRST (run.py:320). Therefore the outer loop can only ever re-run _ensure_media (the media UPLOAD).
findings.sibling_parity: VERIFIED — zernio.py:236-268 is byte-for-byte symmetric with postiz.py:393-428.

findings.title: NON-DEFECT — the requeue family cannot strand a real-sid post
findings.hypothesis_tested: a Studio requeue of a failed post that carries a real submission_id would put it back in `queued`, where _publish_one's skip-resubmit (run.py:287) would strand it in `submitting`.
findings.verdict: DISCONFIRMED. ALL FIVE requeue paths clear submission_id: run.py:423 (daemon), actions.py:1001 (rate-limit), :1032 (oversize), :1063 (transient), :1104 (recover_posts).
findings.but: the strand IS reachable — by a DIFFERENT door. bulk_send_to_review DELIBERATELY preserves submission_id ('Don't touch submission_id / batch_id — keep the lineage'), and Ledger.approve_post (ledger.py:591) does not clear it. See C3-F1.
findings.method_note: This is why a hypothesis must be TESTED, not assumed. The obvious door was closed; the real one was elsewhere.
